steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: "Main idea" means the point the entire passage is making, not just one detail. The passage introduces thistles as unwanted weeds, then immediately pivots: "Although thistles are unwelcome in gardens and on farms, weeds like this do provide some benefits." The main idea must capture that concessive turn.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — Some weeds can be beneficial.: This directly captures the passage's central claim — the "although" pivot from "unwelcome" to "do provide some benefits," illustrated by the pollinator example. ✓
  • Choice B — A variety of plants grow in North America.: The passage mentions thistles are found in North America, but that geographic detail is background, not the point being made. ✗
  • Choice C — Removing weeds is a pleasant task.: The passage says nothing about the experience of removing weeds. This is not supported by any part of the text. ✗
  • Choice D — Some gardening techniques may increase weed growth.: The passage does not discuss gardening techniques at all. This is entirely outside the scope of the text. ✗
That is the correct answer.
